title: Trump 48-Hour Hormuz Ultimatum
market_impact: Escalation risk bullish for oil (CL) and gold (GC). SPY bearish on military action risk. Energy sector (XLE, USO) sensitive.
notes: Posted Mar 21 7:44 PM ET. Deadline Mar 23 7:44 PM ET. Threatened to obliterate Iranian power plants starting with largest.

title: US weighs tough options regarding Iran
market_impact: Elevates geopolitical risk that may boost oil and defense stocks and lift safe-haven flows; watch majors and energy ETFs (XOM, CVX, XLE).

title: US rolls back sanctions on Iran
market_impact: Sanctions rollback could ease oil supply constraints and pressure oil names and commodity ETFs (XOM, CVX, USO) while affecting banks with MENA exposure.

title: US team plans for potential Iran peace talks
market_impact: Prospect of talks reduces tail risk, likely easing pressure on oil and defense names (XOM, XLE, LMT) and dampening safe-haven flows.

title: Trump threatens strikes on Iran over Hormuz blockade
market_impact: Direct threats to infrastructure and the Strait risk immediate spikes in Brent/WTI and lift energy and defense stocks (USO, XOM, XLE, LMT).

title: Trump gives Iran 48 hours on Hormuz, threatens power plants
market_impact: 48-hour ultimatum heightens immediate risk of supply disruption; expect sharp moves in oil (USO, XOM, XLE) and defense contractors (LMT, NOC).

title: US warns it will 'obliterate' Iranian power plants
market_impact: Explicit threat to infrastructure raises odds of regional escalation and supply shocks, boosting oil and defense equities (USO, XOM, LMT).

title: US signals easing sanctions while adding troops to Iran
market_impact: Conflicting signals (sanctions easing vs. troop increases) create market volatility — likely moving oil, defense, and bank stocks (XOM, LMT, JPM).

title: US lifts sanctions on millions of barrels of Iranian oil
market_impact: Lifting sanctions increases available supply and likely eases upward pressure on global crude and benefits refiners; monitor WTI/Brent futures and major energy names.

title: Iran war escalates beyond US control
market_impact: Military escalation raises geopolitical risk and is likely to push up crude prices and boost safe-haven assets while weighing on equities.

title: US warns it may obliterate Iran power plants
market_impact: Threats to critical infrastructure and the Strait of Hormuz heighten the risk of oil supply disruption, likely spiking crude and boosting energy and safe-haven assets.

title: US threatens attacks on Iran power plants; 48-hour ultimatum
market_impact: A specific 48-hour ultimatum creates immediate risk of strikes that could disrupt Strait of Hormuz shipping and spike crude, prompting volatile moves in energy and safe-haven markets.

title: US rushes to contain rising oil and gas prices amid Iran war
market_impact: Government moves to limit fuel price rises could cap upside in oil and gas prices and influence energy sector earnings and refining margins.

title: US weighing deployment of ground troops to Iran
market_impact: Consideration of ground troop deployment would mark a major escalation, likely lifting oil and safe-haven assets and pressuring equities and risk-sensitive sectors.

title: Trump considers winding down Iran war, Hormuz unopened
market_impact: Signals the conflict may continue with the Strait of Hormuz closed, likely supporting higher crude prices and boosting energy producers (XOM, CVX) and energy ETFs (XLE, USO).

title: US explores framework for potential Iran peace talks
market_impact: Reports of potential peace talks are de-escalatory and could pressure oil prices lower while easing risk premia on energy names and regional assets (XOM, CVX, XLE, USO).

market_impact: A plan to seize Kharg Island would sharply raise risk to Middle East oil flows and likely spike WTI/Brent, benefiting energy producers (XOM, CVX) and oil ETFs (USO, XLE) while pressuring transport/reinsurance sectors.

title: US threatens to target Iran energy sites; 48-hour ultimatum
market_impact: An explicit 48-hour threat to Iran's energy infrastructure is highly market-moving and would likely cause immediate crude price spikes and volatility in energy stocks and ETFs (XOM, CVX, XLE, USO).

market_impact: Congress pushing for an exit plan could shorten the conflict horizon, affecting defense contractors (LMT, NOC, RTX) and energy market risk premia tied to prolonged hostilities.

Adjacent quantum names (IONQ/RGTI/QBTS) failed to sustain impulse rally. Watch SPCX day-1 reaction Jun 15-16 — if it follows QNT pattern (open green, close flat, break IPO by day-3), R487 macro demand shock is fully active and IPO-funded liquidity drain is the dominant flow.
notes: Closed prices via yfinance Jun 9 EOD: 6/4=$60.38 6/5=$56.26 6/8=$58.40 6/9=$54.94. Day-1 high=$71.35, day-2 low=$51.15. IPO price=$60. Vol Jun 4=29.6M → Jun 9=4.5M (interest collapsing 85% in 3 sessions).
